Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HEATHER GREGG VS. JAMES J. CUNNINGHAM ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 14, 2023 line 4. DEFENDANT JAMES CUNNINGHAM DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. Defendant's request for judicial notice is GRANTED. The Court will take notice of the filings, they legal effect and existence statements/omissions contained therein. Judicial notice is not taken of veracity of any statements contained therein, unless they relevant finding of law or fact contained in a final court order order/judgment. As to the cause of action for quiet title and related relief contained in the cause of action for declaratory relief, demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to reconcile her claims to the property with the lack of such claims in prior judicial proceedings. Plaintiff must also determine weather Plaintiff intends to proceed with this claim in personam in this jurisdiction or in rem in Massachusetts. This court will not proceed with this action for a personal judgment which will later have to be enforced in Massachusetts if Plaintiff is at the same time proceeding in Massachusetts. If Plaintiff decides to maintain an action in Massachusetts, Plaintiff shall not re-allege these claims in the amended complaint. If Plaintiff dismisses the action in Massachusetts, Plaintiff shall file an amended complaint consistent with this ruling. As to the remainder of the cause of action for declaratory relief Plaintiff shall specify the assets referenced in this cause of action but not listed. If this cause of action is limited to the two assets specifically listed therein, amended complaint shall so allege. Furthermore, Plaintiff shall also allege facts supporting the claim to interest in Twyford Abbey, LLC July 2016 investment (an asset) in light of the judicially noticeable documents. As to the causes of action for promissory estopel, express and implied contract, the demurrer is sustained with leave to amend. Complaint sufficiently asserts alternative theories for recovery. Complaint also sufficiently asserts facts to support some consideration by Plaintiff. However, Complaint does not sufficiently state the terms of the contract/promises to be enforced. Plaintiff shall have leave to amend to allege the formation of the contract(s) and the exact terms of the contract(s). Plaintiff shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
